title: Mors Janua
content:
Pilgrim, no shrine is here, no prison, no inn:
            Thy fear and thy belief alike are fond:
        Death is a gate, and holds no room within:
            Pass--to the road beyond.
meaning:
"Mors Janua" is a quintessential example of Henry John Newbolt, Sir's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
